allies.rationale_text: Hindsight bias is powered by the brain's narrative-construction machinery — once an outcome is known, the causal story to that outcome is assembled retroactively and experienced as the real pre-existing story, making outcomes feel inevitable through imposed causal structure.
allies.source_quote: Hindsight bias is powered by the brain's narrative-construction machinery: once an outcome is known, the causal story is assembled retroactively and experienced as the real pre-existing story.

allies.rationale_text: After outcomes are known, confirmation bias selectively recalls and weights pre-event signals consistent with the outcome while signals pointing elsewhere fade — hindsight bias provides the retrospective direction and confirmation bias selectively retrieves evidence to support it.
allies.source_quote: Confirmation bias selectively recalls pre-event signals consistent with the known outcome while signals pointing elsewhere fade.

allies.rationale_text: Optimism bias and hindsight bias jointly distort temporal reasoning — optimism over-optimistically projects futures while hindsight over-deterministically reconstructs pasts, producing a decision environment where the future feels more controllable and the past more predictable than either actually was.
allies.source_quote: Both optimism bias and hindsight bias distort temporal reasoning: optimism bias projects futures over-optimistically; hindsight bias reconstructs pasts over-deterministically.

antagonists.rationale_text: Probabilistic thinking requires retaining the uncertainty that existed at decision time — the distribution of possible outcomes, the genuine ambiguity of the signals — which hindsight bias collapses onto the realized outcome.
antagonists.source_quote: Probabilistic thinking requires retaining the uncertainty that existed at decision time; hindsight bias collapses that distribution onto the realized outcome.

antagonists.rationale_text: The pre-mortem is a prospective technique that generates exactly the kind of pre-event uncertainty documentation that makes hindsight bias difficult to apply — by inhabiting possible futures before they occur, it preserves the genuine uncertainty that hindsight bias would otherwise collapse.
antagonists.source_quote: The pre-mortem preserves the genuine uncertainty that hindsight bias would otherwise collapse by documenting prospective failure scenarios before outcomes are known.

antagonists.rationale_text: Base rates provide the objective probabilistic scaffolding that hindsight bias erodes — what was the prior probability of this outcome based on the reference class of similar events provides an anchor against which hindsight-contaminated assessments can be measured.
antagonists.source_quote: Base rates provide the objective probabilistic scaffolding that hindsight bias erodes.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Hindsight-bias conflicts with probabilistic-thinking when the known outcome collapses the decision-time probability distribution into retrospective certainty, corrupting calibration and making good decisions under uncertainty look like bad decisions.
structured_tensions.source_quote: hindsight-bias vs probabilistic-thinking: hindsight-bias conflicts with probabilistic-thinking (probabilistic thinking) when the known outcome collapses the decision-time probability distribution into retrospective certainty.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Hindsight-bias conflicts with pre-mortem when post-event retrospective review replaces the pre-event prospective reasoning that the pre-mortem technique is designed to document and protect.
structured_tensions.source_quote: hindsight-bias vs pre-mortem: hindsight-bias conflicts with pre-mortem (pre-mortem) when post-event retrospective review replaces the pre-event prospective reasoning.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Hindsight-bias conflicts with base-rates when retrospective outcome knowledge makes a previously uncertain event feel inevitable, suppressing the accurate prior probability that base-rate reasoning would have assigned.
structured_tensions.source_quote: hindsight-bias vs base-rates: hindsight-bias conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when retrospective outcome knowledge makes a previously uncertain event feel inevitable.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Hindsight-bias conflicts with scientific-method-evidence-testing when post-hoc outcome knowledge contaminates hypothesis assessment, making results that were not predicted appear to have been anticipated all along.
structured_tensions.source_quote: hindsight-bias vs scientific-method-evidence-testing: hindsight-bias conflicts with scientific-method-evidence-testing (scientific method evidence testing) when post-hoc outcome knowledge contaminates hypothesis assessment.

curation_notes.summary: Hindsight bias Wave 3 curated from new source markdown. Key allies: narratives (retroactive story construction), confirmation-bias (selective recall), optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy (paired temporal distortion). Key antagonists: probabilistic-thinking, premortem, base-rates. Four structured tensions.
